title: lophophora diffusa subsp. fricii
intro: Lophophora diffusa subsp. fricii, also known as the Fric's peyote, is a rare and lesser-known cactus species belonging to the Lophophora genus. It is native to the Chihuahuan Desert and is prized for its psychoactive and medicinal properties, particularly the presence of mescaline, a naturally occurring alkaloid. The health benefits of Lophophora diffusa subsp. fricii include its potential to alleviate anxiety, depression, and pain, while also promoting spiritual growth and introspection. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and powdering the cactus, which can then be used in teas, tinctures, or capsules under the guidance of a medical professional.
